key_risks: A strong jobs report or benign FOMC minutes could reignite mega-cap tech leadership, causing this rotation-tilted portfolio to lag SPY.
Recent 30-day strength in healthcare, biotech, and financials could mean-revert; independent catalyst support beyond price action is limited.
Hot wage or inflation data could push yields higher, hurting biotech and value equities alike.
Biotech's very high idiosyncratic risk (clinical/regulatory news) could drag returns in a short window.

portfolio.rationale: Broad 30-day strength with low beta (0.37) and defensive character supports continuation into a data-heavy week; sector rotation toward defensives amid sticky 4.1% PCE inflation.
Strong recent breadth (66.7% up days, near 52w high) with healthcare sector rotation support; higher beta captures upside if risk appetite holds.
Value/financials rotation evident (+6.9% 30d, 61.9% up days) with upcoming bank earnings anticipation and moderate beta; sticky inflation and 4.48% 10y support net interest margins.
Value outperforming growth amid sticky inflation; lower volatility and near 52w highs, benefits from broadening market breadth.
Most stocks rising while mega-cap tech lags; equal-weight captures breadth broadening at new highs with low drawdown.

portfolio_rationale: Rotation away from stretched mega-cap tech into value, financials, healthcare and biotech is supported by breadth data, sticky inflation, and defensive positioning ahead of the jobs report, FOMC minutes, and a holiday-shortened week.
rationale_summary: Overweight healthcare/biotech, financials, and value/equal-weight to ride the ongoing breadth rotation while limiting exposure to faltering AI/tech leadership over the short window.
